headline: Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
description: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

headline: US and Iran escalate 'double blockade' of Strait of Hormuz
Zelensky condemns Russia's deadly strikes in Ukraine as Moscow seeks peace for May 9 parade
Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Mohammadi 'between life and death,' her lawyer says
description: Narges Mohammadi had two suspected heart attacks on March 24 and May 1 in prison in northern Iran, according to her supporters.

headline: Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
description: The war in the Middle East has caused the price of jet fuel to double in just a few weeks. Many airlines, especially low-cost carriers, have canceled flights with low occupancy or low profitability. Passenger rights are protected, but within certain limits.

headline: 'Vincent Bolloré's strategy is to take over the media and publishers to turn them into propaganda tools'
description: Sociologist Gisèle Sapiro rejects the idea that the dismissal of Grasset publisher Olivier Nora is a mere 'epiphenomena in the history of financial capitalism' and stresses the political and ideological threat that this decision poses.

headline: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
description: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces fourth presidential run
description: Despite the emergence of several figures within La France Insoumise and his promise to step aside after the 2022 election, in which he gathered 21.95% of the vote, the populist leader announced his candidacy on Sunday.

headline: The 'alpha male' holds little appeal for women (and comes at a high cost)
description: While many young men are receptive to the hyper-masculine rhetoric that is spread across social media, the stereotypes touted as ways to attract women rarely succeed, writes columnist Maïa Mazaurette. Instead, she encourages men to take a genuine interest in women.

headline: Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
description: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.
